vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is partially accurate but shows significant decline. P1 (String of Nickels) is present in the yellow pot. P3 (Pothos) is present with the rabbit anchor. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) appear to be in the lower black pot, though the specimen count is critically low.

inventory_reconciliation.P4: Present (Lower Black Pot - single leaf remaining)

plant_audit.P1: Stable, showing consistent leaf structure in the yellow pot.
plant_audit.P2: Systemic Loss; no evidence of Mexican Mint foliage.
plant_audit.P3: Stressed; one necrotic leaf (yellowing/browning) and one viable green leaf.
plant_audit.P4: Critical; only one singular leaf remains in the lower pot, appearing isolated.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently damp/dark; no signs of surface cracking.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: Presence of white debris (perlite or eggshell fragments) in P3 and P4; electronic wiring visible near P4.

temporal_deltas.methodology: I first performed a frame-by-frame visual scan from EARLIEST to CURRENT, noting the degradation of the P3 leaf and the lack of new growth in P4. I then validated this by comparing the pixel density of the green foliage across the timeline.
temporal_deltas.observations: The P3 leaf has maintained a steady state of necrosis (yellowing) over the 5-day period. The P4 specimen has remained static with no new growth or further decay, suggesting a state of dormancy or terminal decline.

vision_report.anomalies: The P3 rabbit anchor remains stable, but the surrounding soil shows no signs of new root activity or secondary growth.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently in a state of low-activity maintenance. P1 remains the most robust specimen. P3 is exhibiting clear signs of physiological stress, evidenced by the progressive yellowing of the primary leaf. P4 is in a precarious state, reduced to a single leaf with no signs of propagation or recovery. The environment is stable but the biological occupants are struggling.
